title: Anthonotha fragrans
taxonomy: Anthonotha fragrans is classified as a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. This plant is native to tropical Africa and is known for its fragrant flowers. Anthonotha fragrans is a member of the genus Anthonotha, which is a group of flowering plants. The classification of Anthonotha fragrans at the higher taxonomic levels is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Fabales, family Fabaceae, genus Anthonotha, species A. fragrans. Further classification at the infraspecific level is not available for this species.
uses: Anthonotha fragrans has been found to have various medicinal uses in traditional African medicine. Its bark and leaves are used to treat fever, malaria, and other infectious diseases. The plant's extracts are also used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. In addition, it is used to treat respiratory problems like bronchitis and asthma. Furthermore, its antiseptic properties make it useful in wound healing and preventing infection.
benefits: Anthonotha fragrans is known for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. It also has antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of its health benefits.

The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant's flavonoids have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
